## Python source for reference (exact logic to port):

```python
def infer_client(client: str, source: Path) -> str:
    if client != "auto":
        return client
    normalized = source.as_posix().lower()
    if "/.codex/" in normalized or "rollout-" in source.name.lower():
        return "codex"
    if "/.claude/" in normalized or "/projects/" in normalized:
        return "claude"
    if "/.gemini/" in normalized or "checkpoint" in normalized:
        return "gemini"
    raise SystemExit(f"could not infer transcript client from source path: {source}")

def parse_codex_transcript(path: Path) -> ParsedTranscript:
    records = load_jsonl(path)
    session_id = path.stem
    title = path.stem
    started_at = normalize_timestamp(path.stat().st_mtime)
    cwd = ""
    branch = ""
    messages = []
    tool_events = []

    for record in records:
        record_type = str(record.get("type") or "")
        timestamp = normalize_timestamp(record.get("timestamp"), started_at)
        if record_type == "session_meta":
            payload = ensure_dict(record.get("payload"))
            session_id = str(payload.get("id") or session_id)
            title = str(payload.get("thread_name") or payload.get("title") or title)
            started_at = earliest_timestamp(started_at, payload.get("timestamp"))
            cwd = str(payload.get("cwd") or cwd)
            branch = str(get_nested(payload, "git", "branch") or branch)
            continue
        if record_type not in {"response_item", "event_msg"}:
            continue
        payload = ensure_dict(record.get("payload"))
        payload_type = str(payload.get("type") or "")
        if payload_type == "message":
            role = str(payload.get("role") or "").lower()
            if role in {"user", "assistant"}:
                text = extract_text_blocks(payload.get("content"))
                if text:
                    messages.append(TranscriptMessage(role=role, text=text, timestamp=timestamp))
            continue
        if payload_type in {"user_message", "agent_message"}:
            role = "user" if payload_type == "user_message" else "assistant"
            text = str(payload.get("message") or "").strip()
            if text:
                messages.append(TranscriptMessage(role=role, text=text, timestamp=timestamp))
            continue
        if payload_type == "function_call":
            name = str(payload.get("name") or "function_call")
            arguments = payload.get("arguments") or payload.get("input")
            tool_events.append(TranscriptToolEvent(summary=f"{name} input={summarize_value(arguments)}", timestamp=timestamp))
            continue
        if payload_type == "custom_tool_call":
            name = str(payload.get("name") or payload.get("call_id") or "custom_tool_call")
            tool_events.append(TranscriptToolEvent(summary=f"{name} input={summarize_value(payload.get('input'))}", timestamp=timestamp))
            continue
        if payload_type == "function_call_output":
            name = str(payload.get("name") or payload.get("call_id") or "function_call_output")
            output = payload.get("output") or payload.get("content")
            tool_events.append(TranscriptToolEvent(summary=f"{name} output={summarize_value(output)}", timestamp=timestamp))
            continue
        if payload_type == "custom_tool_call_output":
            name = str(payload.get("name") or payload.get("call_id") or "custom_tool_call_output")
            output = payload.get("output") or payload.get("content")
            tool_events.append(TranscriptToolEvent(summary=f"{name} output={summarize_value(output)}", timestamp=timestamp))
    messages = deduplicate_messages(messages)
    tool_events = deduplicate_tool_events(tool_events)
    return ParsedTranscript(client="codex", session_id=session_id, title=title, started_at=started_at, cwd=cwd, branch=branch, messages=messages, tool_events=tool_events, source_path=path)

def parse_claude_transcript(path: Path) -> ParsedTranscript:
    records = load_jsonl(path)
    session_id = path.stem
    title = path.stem
    started_at = normalize_timestamp(path.stat().st_mtime)
    cwd = ""
    branch = ""
    messages = []
    tool_events = []
    for record in records:
        record_type = str(record.get("type") or "")
        timestamp = normalize_timestamp(record.get("timestamp"), started_at)
        session_id = str(record.get("sessionId") or session_id)
        started_at = earliest_timestamp(started_at, record.get("timestamp"))
        cwd = str(record.get("cwd") or cwd)
        branch = str(record.get("gitBranch") or branch)
        if record_type not in {"user", "assistant"}:
            continue
        message = ensure_dict(record.get("message"))
        role = str(message.get("role") or record_type).lower()
        text_parts = []
        content = message.get("content")
        if isinstance(content, str):
            text = content.strip()
            if role in {"user", "assistant"} and text:
                messages.append(TranscriptMessage(role=role, text=text, timestamp=timestamp))
            continue
        for block in ensure_list(content):
            if isinstance(block, str):
                if block.strip():
                    text_parts.append(block.strip())
                continue
            if not isinstance(block, dict):
                continue
            block_type = str(block.get("type") or "").lower()
            if block_type in TEXT_BLOCK_TYPES:
                text = str(block.get("text") or "").strip()
                if text:
                    text_parts.append(text)
                continue
            if block_type in SKIP_BLOCK_TYPES:
                continue
            if block_type in TOOL_USE_TYPES:
                name = str(block.get("name") or "tool_use")
                tool_events.append(TranscriptToolEvent(summary=f"{name} input={summarize_value(block.get('input'))}", timestamp=timestamp))
                continue
            if block_type in TOOL_RESULT_TYPES:
                tool_use_id = str(block.get("tool_use_id") or "tool_result")
                tool_events.append(TranscriptToolEvent(summary=f"{tool_use_id} output={summarize_value(block.get('content'))}", timestamp=timestamp))
        text = join_paragraphs(text_parts)
        if role in {"user", "assistant"} and text:
            messages.append(TranscriptMessage(role=role, text=text, timestamp=timestamp))
    messages = deduplicate_messages(messages)
    tool_events = deduplicate_tool_events(tool_events)
    return ParsedTranscript(client="claude", ...)

def parse_gemini_transcript(path: Path) -> ParsedTranscript:
    session_id = path.stem
    title = path.stem
    started_at = normalize_timestamp(path.stat().st_mtime)
    cwd = ""
    branch = ""
    messages = []
    tool_events = []
    if path.suffix.lower() == ".jsonl":
        payload = load_jsonl(path)
    else:
        payload = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    first_meta = find_first_mapping_with_keys(payload, {"sessionId", "chatId", "cwd", "branch", "timestamp"})
    if first_meta:
        session_id = str(first_meta.get("sessionId") or first_meta.get("chatId") or first_meta.get("id") or session_id)
        started_at = earliest_timestamp(started_at, first_meta.get("timestamp"))
        cwd = str(first_meta.get("cwd") or first_meta.get("projectRoot") or cwd)
        branch = str(first_meta.get("branch") or branch)
    def visit(node):
        nonlocal session_id, started_at, cwd, branch
        if isinstance(node, list):
            for item in node: visit(item)
            return
        if not isinstance(node, dict):
            return
        session_id = str(node.get("sessionId") or node.get("chatId") or node.get("id") or session_id)
        started_at = earliest_timestamp(started_at, node.get("timestamp"))
        cwd = str(node.get("cwd") or node.get("projectRoot") or cwd)
        branch = str(node.get("branch") or branch)
        role = str(node.get("role") or node.get("author") or node.get("sender") or "").lower()
        timestamp = normalize_timestamp(node.get("timestamp"), started_at)
        if role in {"user", "assistant", "model"}:
            normalized_role = "assistant" if role == "model" else role
            text = extract_gemini_text(node)
            if text:
                messages.append(TranscriptMessage(role=normalized_role, text=text, timestamp=timestamp))
        tool_name = (get_nested(node, "toolUse", "name") or node.get("toolName") or node.get("functionName") or node.get("tool"))
        if tool_name:
            tool_events.append(TranscriptToolEvent(summary=f"{tool_name} input={summarize_value(node.get('args') or node.get('input'))}", timestamp=timestamp))
        for value in node.values():
            visit(value)
    visit(payload)
    messages = deduplicate_messages(messages)
    tool_events = deduplicate_tool_events(tool_events)
    return ParsedTranscript(client="gemini", ...)
```

## `src/transcript/db.ts` should export:

`upsertSearchIndex(dbPath: string, manifest: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<void>` — async because sql.js WASM initialization is async.

Implementation:
1. Initialize sql.js with `initSqlJs()` (cache the promise for reuse)
2. If db file exists at dbPath, read it and open with `new SQL.Database(fileBuffer)`, else create new `new SQL.Database()`
3. Run `PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL`
4. Create table `transcripts` if not exists with 18 columns:
   - client, project, session_id, raw_sha256, title, started_at, imported_at
   - cwd, branch, raw_source_path, raw_upload_permission
   - global_raw_path, global_clean_path, repo_raw_path, repo_clean_path, repo_manifest_path
   - message_count (INTEGER), tool_event_count (INTEGER)
   - PRIMARY KEY (client, project, session_id, raw_sha256)
5. INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE SET (all 14 non-PK data columns)
6. Export and write: `db.export()` returns Uint8Array → `writeFileSync(dbPath, Buffer.from(data))`
7. Close the database

Here's the Python source:

```python
def upsert_search_index(db_path: Path, manifest: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)
    try:
        conn.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL")
        conn.execute("""
            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS transcripts (
                client TEXT NOT NULL,
                project TEXT NOT NULL,
                session_id TEXT NOT NULL,
                raw_sha256 TEXT NOT NULL,
                title TEXT NOT NULL,
                started_at TEXT NOT NULL,
                imported_at TEXT NOT NULL,
                cwd TEXT NOT NULL,
                branch TEXT NOT NULL,
                raw_source_path TEXT NOT NULL,
                raw_upload_permission TEXT NOT NULL,
                global_raw_path TEXT NOT NULL,
                global_clean_path TEXT NOT NULL,
                repo_raw_path TEXT NOT NULL,
                repo_clean_path TEXT NOT NULL,
                repo_manifest_path TEXT NOT NULL,
                message_count INTEGER NOT NULL,
                tool_event_count INTEGER NOT NULL,
                PRIMARY KEY (client, project, session_id, raw_sha256)
            )
        """)
        conn.execute("""
            INSERT INTO transcripts (
                client, project, session_id, raw_sha256, title, started_at, imported_at,
                cwd, branch, raw_source_path, raw_upload_permission,
                global_raw_path, global_clean_path, repo_raw_path, repo_clean_path, repo_manifest_path,
                message_count, tool_event_count
            ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
            ON CONFLICT(client, project, session_id, raw_sha256) DO UPDATE SET
                title = excluded.title,
                started_at = excluded.started_at,
                imported_at = excluded.imported_at,
                cwd = excluded.cwd,
                branch = excluded.branch,
                raw_source_path = excluded.raw_source_path,
                raw_upload_permission = excluded.raw_upload_permission,
                global_raw_path = excluded.global_raw_path,
                global_clean_path = excluded.global_clean_path,
                repo_raw_path = excluded.repo_raw_path,
                repo_clean_path = excluded.repo_clean_path,
                repo_manifest_path = excluded.repo_manifest_path,
                message_count = excluded.message_count,
                tool_event_count = excluded.tool_event_count
        """, (manifest["client"], manifest["project"], manifest["session_id"], manifest["raw_sha256"],
              manifest["title"], manifest["started_at"], manifest["imported_at"],
              manifest["cwd"], manifest["branch"], manifest["raw_source_path"], manifest["raw_upload_permission"],
              manifest["global_raw_path"], manifest["global_clean_path"],
              manifest["repo_raw_path"], manifest["repo_clean_path"], manifest["repo_manifest_path"],
              manifest["message_count"], manifest["tool_event_count"]))
        conn.commit()
    finally:
        conn.close()
```

## `src/transcript/discover.ts` should export:

1. `defaultGlobalTranscriptRoot(): string` — returns `~/.memorytree/transcripts` (use `os.homedir()`)

2. `defaultClientRoots(): Record<string, string>` — returns `{ codex: ~/.codex, claude: ~/.claude, gemini: ~/.gemini }`

3. `discoverSourceFiles(clients?: ReadonlySet<string>): Array<[string, string]>` — Scans for transcript files:
   - Use `clients ?? CLIENTS` for requested set
   - For each client, check if root directory exists (use `existsSync`)
   - Glob patterns per client:
     - codex: `sessions/**/*.jsonl`
     - claude: `projects/**/*.jsonl`
     - gemini: `tmp/*/checkpoints/**/*.json`, `tmp/*/checkpoints/**/*.jsonl`, `history/**/*.json`, `history/**/*.jsonl`, `chats/**/*.json`, `chats/**/*.jsonl`
   - Use `fg.sync(pattern, { cwd: root, absolute: true, onlyFiles: true })` for each pattern
   - Deduplicate by resolved lowercase posix path
   - Sort by mtime descending (use `safeFileMtime`)
   - Return array of `[client, absolutePosixPath]` tuples

4. `inferProjectSlug(parsed: ParsedTranscript): string` — Derive project slug:
   - If parsed.cwd: try to get basename of cwd, slugify it with fallback 'project'
   - If parsed.client === 'claude': get parent dir name from source_path, strip `^[a-z]--` prefix, slugify with fallback 'project'
   - If parsed.client === 'gemini' and parent dir exists: slugify parent dir name
   - Fallback: 'unknown-project'

5. `transcriptMatchesRepo(parsed: ParsedTranscript, repoRoot: string, repoSlug: string): boolean` — Check match:
   - Resolve and normalize both repoRoot and parsed.cwd to posix
   - If cwd === repoRoot or repoRoot is prefix of cwd (parent dir): return true
   - Else check projectSlugsMatch(inferProjectSlug(parsed), repoSlug)

6. `projectSlugsMatch(left: string, right: string): boolean` — Both non-empty, equal, not 'unknown-project'

7. `safeFileMtime(filePath: string): number` — Returns mtime in seconds (statSync(filePath).mtimeMs / 1000), or 0 on error

## Python source:

```python
def default_global_transcript_root() -> Path:
    return Path.home() / ".memorytree" / "transcripts"

def default_client_roots() -> dict[str, Path]:
    home = Path.home()
    return {"codex": home / ".codex", "claude": home / ".claude", "gemini": home / ".gemini"}

def discover_source_files(clients: set[str] | None = None) -> list[tuple[str, Path]]:
    requested = clients or CLIENTS
    roots = default_client_roots()
    patterns = {
        "codex": ["sessions/**/*.jsonl"],
        "claude": ["projects/**/*.jsonl"],
        "gemini": ["tmp/*/checkpoints/**/*.json", "tmp/*/checkpoints/**/*.jsonl",
                   "history/**/*.json", "history/**/*.jsonl", "chats/**/*.json", "chats/**/*.jsonl"],
    }
    matches = []
    seen = set()
    for client in sorted(requested):
        root = roots.get(client)
        if root is None or not root.exists():
            continue
        for pattern in patterns[client]:
            for path in root.glob(pattern):
                if not path.is_file():
                    continue
                key = path.resolve().as_posix().lower()
                if key in seen:
                    continue
                seen.add(key)
                matches.append((client, path.resolve()))
    matches.sort(key=lambda item: (safe_file_mtime(item[1]), item[1].as_posix().lower()), reverse=True)
    return matches

def infer_project_slug(parsed: ParsedTranscript) -> str:
    if parsed.cwd:
        try:
            cwd_path = Path(parsed.cwd)
            return slugify(cwd_path.name, fallback="project")
        except OSError:
            pass
    if parsed.client == "claude":
        parent_name = parsed.source_path.parent.name
        parent_name = re.sub(r"^[a-z]--", "", parent_name.lower())
        return slugify(parent_name, fallback="project")
    if parsed.client == "gemini" and parsed.source_path.parent.name:
        return slugify(parsed.source_path.parent.name, fallback="project")
    return "unknown-project"

def transcript_matches_repo(parsed: ParsedTranscript, repo_root: Path, repo_slug: str) -> bool:
    repo_root = repo_root.resolve()
    if parsed.cwd:
        try:
            cwd_path = Path(parsed.cwd).resolve()
            if cwd_path == repo_root:
                return True
            if repo_root in cwd_path.parents:
                return True
        except OSError:
            pass
    return project_slugs_match(infer_project_slug(parsed), repo_slug)

def project_slugs_match(left: str, right: str) -> bool:
    return bool(left and right and left == right and left != "unknown-project")

def safe_file_mtime(path: Path) -> float:
    try:
        return path.stat().st_mtime
    except OSError:
        return 0.0
```

   - Detect roles: user/assistant/model (model → assistant)
   - Detect tools: toolUse.name, toolName, functionName, tool
   - Deduplicate before returning

## Python source for reference (exact logic to port):

```python
def infer_client(client: str, source: Path) -> str:
    if client != "auto":
        return client
    normalized = source.as_posix().lower()
    if "/.codex/" in normalized or "rollout-" in source.name.lower():
        return "codex"
    if "/.claude/" in normalized or "/projects/" in normalized:
        return "claude"
    if "/.gemini/" in normalized or "checkpoint" in normalized:
        return "gemini"
    raise SystemExit(f"could not infer transcript client from source path: {source}")

def parse_codex_transcript(path: Path) -> ParsedTranscript:
    records = load_jsonl(path)
    session_id = path.stem
    title = path.stem
    started_at = normalize_timestamp(path.stat().st_mtime)
    cwd = ""
    branch = ""
    messages = []
    tool_events = []

    for record in records:
        record_type = str(record.get("type") or "")
        timestamp = normalize_timestamp(record.get("timestamp"), started_at)
        if record_type == "session_meta":
            payload = ensure_dict(record.get("payload"))
            session_id = str(payload.get("id") or session_id)
            title = str(payload.get("thread_name") or payload.get("title") or title)
            started_at = earliest_timestamp(started_at, payload.get("timestamp"))
            cwd = str(payload.get("cwd") or cwd)
            branch = str(get_nested(payload, "git", "branch") or branch)
            continue
        if record_type not in {"response_item", "event_msg"}:
            continue
        payload = ensure_dict(record.get("payload"))
        payload_type = str(payload.get("type") or "")
        if payload_type == "message":
            role = str(payload.get("role") or "").lower()
            if role in {"user", "assistant"}:
                text = extract_text_blocks(payload.get("content"))
                if text:
                    messages.append(TranscriptMessage(role=role, text=text, timestamp=timestamp))
            continue
        if payload_type in {"user_message", "agent_message"}:
            role = "user" if payload_type == "user_message" else "assistant"
            text = str(payload.get("message") or "").strip()
            if text:
                messages.append(TranscriptMessage(role=role, text=text, timestamp=timestamp))
            continue
        if payload_type == "function_call":
            name = str(payload.get("name") or "function_call")
            arguments = payload.get("arguments") or payload.get("input")
            tool_events.append(TranscriptToolEvent(summary=f"{name} input={summarize_value(arguments)}", timestamp=timestamp))
            continue
        if payload_type == "custom_tool_call":
            name = str(payload.get("name") or payload.get("call_id") or "custom_tool_call")
            tool_events.append(TranscriptToolEvent(summary=f"{name} input={summarize_value(payload.get('input'))}", timestamp=timestamp))
            continue
        if payload_type == "function_call_output":
            name = str(payload.get("name") or payload.get("call_id") or "function_call_output")
            output = payload.get("output") or payload.get("content")
            tool_events.append(TranscriptToolEvent(summary=f"{name} output={summarize_value(output)}", timestamp=timestamp))
            continue
        if payload_type == "custom_tool_call_output":
            name = str(payload.get("name") or payload.get("call_id") or "custom_tool_call_output")
            output = payload.get("output") or payload.get("content")
            tool_events.append(TranscriptToolEvent(summary=f"{name} output={summarize_value(output)}", timestamp=timestamp))
    messages = deduplicate_messages(messages)
    tool_events = deduplicate_tool_events(tool_events)
    return ParsedTranscript(client="codex", session_id=session_id, title=title, started_at=started_at, cwd=cwd, branch=branch, messages=messages, tool_events=tool_events, source_path=path)

def parse_claude_transcript(path: Path) -> ParsedTranscript:
    records = load_jsonl(path)
    session_id = path.stem
    title = path.stem
    started_at = normalize_timestamp(path.stat().st_mtime)
    cwd = ""
    branch = ""
    messages = []
    tool_events = []
    for record in records:
        record_type = str(record.get("type") or "")
        timestamp = normalize_timestamp(record.get("timestamp"), started_at)
        session_id = str(record.get("sessionId") or session_id)
        started_at = earliest_timestamp(started_at, record.get("timestamp"))
        cwd = str(record.get("cwd") or cwd)
        branch = str(record.get("gitBranch") or branch)
        if record_type not in {"user", "assistant"}:
            continue
        message = ensure_dict(record.get("message"))
        role = str(message.get("role") or record_type).lower()
        text_parts = []
        content = message.get("content")
        if isinstance(content, str):
            text = content.strip()
            if role in {"user", "assistant"} and text:
                messages.append(TranscriptMessage(role=role, text=text, timestamp=timestamp))
            continue
        for block in ensure_list(content):
            if isinstance(block, str):
                if block.strip():
                    text_parts.append(block.strip())
                continue
            if not isinstance(block, dict):
                continue
            block_type = str(block.get("type") or "").lower()
            if block_type in TEXT_BLOCK_TYPES:
                text = str(block.get("text") or "").strip()
                if text:
                    text_parts.append(text)
                continue
            if block_type in SKIP_BLOCK_TYPES:
                continue
            if block_type in TOOL_USE_TYPES:
                name = str(block.get("name") or "tool_use")
                tool_events.append(TranscriptToolEvent(summary=f"{name} input={summarize_value(block.get('input'))}", timestamp=timestamp))
                continue
            if block_type in TOOL_RESULT_TYPES:
                tool_use_id = str(block.get("tool_use_id") or "tool_result")
                tool_events.append(TranscriptToolEvent(summary=f"{tool_use_id} output={summarize_value(block.get('content'))}", timestamp=timestamp))
        text = join_paragraphs(text_parts)
        if role in {"user", "assistant"} and text:
            messages.append(TranscriptMessage(role=role, text=text, timestamp=timestamp))
    messages = deduplicate_messages(messages)
    tool_events = deduplicate_tool_events(tool_events)
    return ParsedTranscript(client="claude", ...)

def parse_gemini_transcript(path: Path) -> ParsedTranscript:
    session_id = path.stem
    title = path.stem
    started_at = normalize_timestamp(path.stat().st_mtime)
    cwd = ""
    branch = ""
    messages = []
    tool_events = []
    if path.suffix.lower() == ".jsonl":
        payload = load_jsonl(path)
    else:
        payload = json.loads(path.read_text(encoding="utf-8"))
    first_meta = find_first_mapping_with_keys(payload, {"sessionId", "chatId", "cwd", "branch", "timestamp"})
    if first_meta:
        session_id = str(first_meta.get("sessionId") or first_meta.get("chatId") or first_meta.get("id") or session_id)
        started_at = earliest_timestamp(started_at, first_meta.get("timestamp"))
        cwd = str(first_meta.get("cwd") or first_meta.get("projectRoot") or cwd)
        branch = str(first_meta.get("branch") or branch)
    def visit(node):
        nonlocal session_id, started_at, cwd, branch
        if isinstance(node, list):
            for item in node: visit(item)
            return
        if not isinstance(node, dict):
            return
        session_id = str(node.get("sessionId") or node.get("chatId") or node.get("id") or session_id)
        started_at = earliest_timestamp(started_at, node.get("timestamp"))
        cwd = str(node.get("cwd") or node.get("projectRoot") or cwd)
        branch = str(node.get("branch") or branch)
        role = str(node.get("role") or node.get("author") or node.get("sender") or "").lower()
        timestamp = normalize_timestamp(node.get("timestamp"), started_at)
        if role in {"user", "assistant", "model"}:
            normalized_role = "assistant" if role == "model" else role
            text = extract_gemini_text(node)
            if text:
                messages.append(TranscriptMessage(role=normalized_role, text=text, timestamp=timestamp))
        tool_name = (get_nested(node, "toolUse", "name") or node.get("toolName") or node.get("functionName") or node.get("tool"))
        if tool_name:
            tool_events.append(TranscriptToolEvent(summary=f"{tool_name} input={summarize_value(node.get('args') or node.get('input'))}", timestamp=timestamp))
        for value in node.values():
            visit(value)
    visit(payload)
    messages = deduplicate_messages(messages)
    tool_events = deduplicate_tool_events(tool_events)
    return ParsedTranscript(client="gemini", ...)
```

## `src/transcript/db.ts` should export:

`upsertSearchIndex(dbPath: string, manifest: Record<string, unknown>): Promise<void>` — async because sql.js WASM initialization is async.

Implementation:
1. Initialize sql.js with `initSqlJs()` (cache the promise for reuse)
2. If db file exists at dbPath, read it and open with `new SQL.Database(fileBuffer)`, else create new `new SQL.Database()`
3. Run `PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL`
4. Create table `transcripts` if not exists with 18 columns:
   - client, project, session_id, raw_sha256, title, started_at, imported_at
   - cwd, branch, raw_source_path, raw_upload_permission
   - global_raw_path, global_clean_path, repo_raw_path, repo_clean_path, repo_manifest_path
   - message_count (INTEGER), tool_event_count (INTEGER)
   - PRIMARY KEY (client, project, session_id, raw_sha256)
5. INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE SET (all 14 non-PK data columns)
6. Export and write: `db.export()` returns Uint8Array → `writeFileSync(dbPath, Buffer.from(data))`
7. Close the database

Here's the Python source:

```python
def upsert_search_index(db_path: Path, manifest: dict[str, Any]) -> None:
    conn = sqlite3.connect(db_path)
    try:
        conn.execute("PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL")
        conn.execute("""
            CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS transcripts (
                client TEXT NOT NULL,
                project TEXT NOT NULL,
                session_id TEXT NOT NULL,
                raw_sha256 TEXT NOT NULL,
                title TEXT NOT NULL,
                started_at TEXT NOT NULL,
                imported_at TEXT NOT NULL,
                cwd TEXT NOT NULL,
                branch TEXT NOT NULL,
                raw_source_path TEXT NOT NULL,
                raw_upload_permission TEXT NOT NULL,
                global_raw_path TEXT NOT NULL,
                global_clean_path TEXT NOT NULL,
                repo_raw_path TEXT NOT NULL,
                repo_clean_path TEXT NOT NULL,
                repo_manifest_path TEXT NOT NULL,
                message_count INTEGER NOT NULL,
                tool_event_count INTEGER NOT NULL,
                PRIMARY KEY (client, project, session_id, raw_sha256)
            )
        """)
        conn.execute("""
            INSERT INTO transcripts (
                client, project, session_id, raw_sha256, title, started_at, imported_at,
                cwd, branch, raw_source_path, raw_upload_permission,
                global_raw_path, global_clean_path, repo_raw_path, repo_clean_path, repo_manifest_path,
                message_count, tool_event_count
            ) VALUES (?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?, ?)
            ON CONFLICT(client, project, session_id, raw_sha256) DO UPDATE SET
                title = excluded.title,
                started_at = excluded.started_at,
                imported_at = excluded.imported_at,
                cwd = excluded.cwd,
                branch = excluded.branch,
                raw_source_path = excluded.raw_source_path,
                raw_upload_permission = excluded.raw_upload_permission,
                global_raw_path = excluded.global_raw_path,
                global_clean_path = excluded.global_clean_path,
                repo_raw_path = excluded.repo_raw_path,
                repo_clean_path = excluded.repo_clean_path,
                repo_manifest_path = excluded.repo_manifest_path,
                message_count = excluded.message_count,
                tool_event_count = excluded.tool_event_count
        """, (manifest["client"], manifest["project"], manifest["session_id"], manifest["raw_sha256"],
              manifest["title"], manifest["started_at"], manifest["imported_at"],
              manifest["cwd"], manifest["branch"], manifest["raw_source_path"], manifest["raw_upload_permission"],
              manifest["global_raw_path"], manifest["global_clean_path"],
              manifest["repo_raw_path"], manifest["repo_clean_path"], manifest["repo_manifest_path"],
              manifest["message_count"], manifest["tool_event_count"]))
        conn.commit()
    finally:
        conn.close()
```

## `src/transcript/discover.ts` should export:

1. `defaultGlobalTranscriptRoot(): string` — returns `~/.memorytree/transcripts` (use `os.homedir()`)

2. `defaultClientRoots(): Record<string, string>` — returns `{ codex: ~/.codex, claude: ~/.claude, gemini: ~/.gemini }`

3. `discoverSourceFiles(clients?: ReadonlySet<string>): Array<[string, string]>` — Scans for transcript files:
   - Use `clients ?? CLIENTS` for requested set
   - For each client, check if root directory exists (use `existsSync`)
   - Glob patterns per client:
     - codex: `sessions/**/*.jsonl`
     - claude: `projects/**/*.jsonl`
     - gemini: `tmp/*/checkpoints/**/*.json`, `tmp/*/checkpoints/**/*.jsonl`, `history/**/*.json`, `history/**/*.jsonl`, `chats/**/*.json`, `chats/**/*.jsonl`
   - Use `fg.sync(pattern, { cwd: root, absolute: true, onlyFiles: true })` for each pattern
   - Deduplicate by resolved lowercase posix path
   - Sort by mtime descending (use `safeFileMtime`)
   - Return array of `[client, absolutePosixPath]` tuples

4. `inferProjectSlug(parsed: ParsedTranscript): string` — Derive project slug:
   - If parsed.cwd: try to get basename of cwd, slugify it with fallback 'project'
   - If parsed.client === 'claude': get parent dir name from source_path, strip `^[a-z]--` prefix, slugify with fallback 'project'
   - If parsed.client === 'gemini' and parent dir exists: slugify parent dir name
   - Fallback: 'unknown-project'

5. `transcriptMatchesRepo(parsed: ParsedTranscript, repoRoot: string, repoSlug: string): boolean` — Check match:
   - Resolve and normalize both repoRoot and parsed.cwd to posix
   - If cwd === repoRoot or repoRoot is prefix of cwd (parent dir): return true
   - Else check projectSlugsMatch(inferProjectSlug(parsed), repoSlug)

6. `projectSlugsMatch(left: string, right: string): boolean` — Both non-empty, equal, not 'unknown-project'

7. `safeFileMtime(filePath: string): number` — Returns mtime in seconds (statSync(filePath).mtimeMs / 1000), or 0 on error

## Python source:

```python
def default_global_transcript_root() -> Path:
    return Path.home() / ".memorytree" / "transcripts"

def default_client_roots() -> dict[str, Path]:
    home = Path.home()
    return {"codex": home / ".codex", "claude": home / ".claude", "gemini": home / ".gemini"}

def discover_source_files(clients: set[str] | None = None) -> list[tuple[str, Path]]:
    requested = clients or CLIENTS
    roots = default_client_roots()
    patterns = {
        "codex": ["sessions/**/*.jsonl"],
        "claude": ["projects/**/*.jsonl"],
        "gemini": ["tmp/*/checkpoints/**/*.json", "tmp/*/checkpoints/**/*.jsonl",
                   "history/**/*.json", "history/**/*.jsonl", "chats/**/*.json", "chats/**/*.jsonl"],
    }
    matches = []
    seen = set()
    for client in sorted(requested):
        root = roots.get(client)
        if root is None or not root.exists():
            continue
        for pattern in patterns[client]:
            for path in root.glob(pattern):
                if not path.is_file():
                    continue
                key = path.resolve().as_posix().lower()
                if key in seen:
                    continue
                seen.add(key)
                matches.append((client, path.resolve()))
    matches.sort(key=lambda item: (safe_file_mtime(item[1]), item[1].as_posix().lower()), reverse=True)
    return matches

def infer_project_slug(parsed: ParsedTranscript) -> str:
    if parsed.cwd:
        try:
            cwd_path = Path(parsed.cwd)
            return slugify(cwd_path.name, fallback="project")
        except OSError:
            pass
    if parsed.client == "claude":
        parent_name = parsed.source_path.parent.name
        parent_name = re.sub(r"^[a-z]--", "", parent_name.lower())
        return slugify(parent_name, fallback="project")
    if parsed.client == "gemini" and parsed.source_path.parent.name:
        return slugify(parsed.source_path.parent.name, fallback="project")
    return "unknown-project"

def transcript_matches_repo(parsed: ParsedTranscript, repo_root: Path, repo_slug: str) -> bool:
    repo_root = repo_root.resolve()
    if parsed.cwd:
        try:
            cwd_path = Path(parsed.cwd).resolve()
            if cwd_path == repo_root:
                return True
            if repo_root in cwd_path.parents:
                return True
        except OSError:
            pass
    return project_slugs_match(infer_project_slug(parsed), repo_slug)

def project_slugs_match(left: str, right: str) -> bool:
    return bool(left and right and left == right and left != "unknown-project")

def safe_file_mtime(path: Path) -> float:
    try:
        return path.stat().st_mtime
    except OSError:
        return 0.0
```

// Types — ParsedTranscript, Message, ToolEvent interfaces
// To be implemented in Phase 1

export type Client = 'codex' | 'claude' | 'gemini'

export interface TranscriptMessage {

content.content:
1→/**
     2→ * Transcript search index — SQLite upsert for the global transcript catalog.
     3→ *
     4→ * Uses sql.js (WebAssembly SQLite) so the module works without a native
     5→ * sqlite3 binding.  The WASM init is async; we cache the resulting factory
     6→ * promise so it is only resolved once per process.
     7→ */
     8→
     9→import initSqlJs, { type SqlJsStatic } from 'sql.js'
    10→import { existsSync, readFileSync, writeFileSync } from 'node:fs'
    11→import type { ManifestEntry } from '../types/transcript.js'
    12→
    13→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    14→// Lazy sql.js initialisation (cached promise)
    15→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    16→
    17→let sqlPromise: Promise<SqlJsStatic> | null = null
    18→
    19→function getSqlJs(): Promise<SqlJsStatic> {
    20→  if (sqlPromise === null) {
    21→    sqlPromise = initSqlJs()
    22→  }
    23→  return sqlPromise
    24→}
    25→
    26→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    27→// Column definitions
    28→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    29→
    30→const PK_COLUMNS = [
    31→  'client',
    32→  'project',
    33→  'session_id',
    34→  'raw_sha256',
    35→] as const
    36→
    37→const DATA_COLUMNS = [
    38→  'title',
    39→  'started_at',
    40→  'imported_at',
    41→  'cwd',
    42→  'branch',
    43→  'raw_source_path',
    44→  'raw_upload_permission',
    45→  'global_raw_path',
    46→  'global_clean_path',
    47→  'repo_raw_path',
    48→  'repo_clean_path',
    49→  'repo_manifest_path',
    50→  'message_count',
    51→  'tool_event_count',
    52→] as const
    53→
    54→const ALL_COLUMNS = [...PK_COLUMNS, ...DATA_COLUMNS] as const
    55→
    56→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    57→// SQL statements (built once)
    58→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    59→
    60→const CREATE_TABLE_SQL = `
    61→  CREATE TABLE IF NOT EXISTS transcripts (
    62→    client              TEXT    NOT NULL,
    63→    project             TEXT    NOT NULL,
    64→    session_id          TEXT    NOT NULL,
    65→    raw_sha256          TEXT    NOT NULL,
    66→    title               TEXT    NOT NULL,
    67→    started_at          TEXT    NOT NULL,
    68→    imported_at         TEXT    NOT NULL,
    69→    cwd                 TEXT    NOT NULL,
    70→    branch              TEXT    NOT NULL,
    71→    raw_source_path     TEXT    NOT NULL,
    72→    raw_upload_permission TEXT  NOT NULL,
    73→    global_raw_path     TEXT    NOT NULL,
    74→    global_clean_path   TEXT    NOT NULL,
    75→    repo_raw_path       TEXT    NOT NULL,
    76→    repo_clean_path     TEXT    NOT NULL,
    77→    repo_manifest_path  TEXT    NOT NULL,
    78→    message_count       INTEGER NOT NULL,
    79→    tool_event_count    INTEGER NOT NULL,
    80→    PRIMARY KEY (client, project, session_id, raw_sha256)
    81→  )
    82→`
    83→
    84→const UPSERT_SQL = `
    85→  INSERT INTO transcripts (
    86→    ${ALL_COLUMNS.join(', ')}
    87→  ) VALUES (${ALL_COLUMNS.map(() => '?').join(', ')})
    88→  ON CONFLICT(${PK_COLUMNS.join(', ')}) DO UPDATE SET
    89→    ${DATA_COLUMNS.map((c) => `${c} = excluded.${c}`).join(',\n    ')}
    90→`
    91→
    92→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    93→// Public API
    94→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    95→
    96→/**
    97→ * Insert or update a single transcript manifest row in the SQLite search
    98→ * index at `dbPath`.  Creates the database and table if they do not exist.
    99→ *
   100→ * The function is async because the first call must initialise the sql.js
   101→ * WASM module.
   102→ */
   103→export async function upsertSearchIndex(
   104→  dbPath: string,
   105→  manifest: ManifestEntry,
   106→): Promise<void> {
   107→  const SQL = await getSqlJs()
   108→
   109→  const db = existsSync(dbPath)
   110→    ? new SQL.Database(readFileSync(dbPath))
   111→    : new SQL.Database()
   112→
   113→  try {
   114→    db.run('PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL')
   115→    db.run(CREATE_TABLE_SQL)
   116→
   117→    const record: Record<string, unknown> = { ...manifest }
   118→    const params = ALL_COLUMNS.map((col) => {
   119→      const value = record[col]
   120→      if (value === undefined || value === null) {
   121→        return ''
   122→      }
   123→      return typeof value === 'number' ? value : String(value)
   124→    })
   125→
   126→    db.run(UPSERT_SQL, params)
   127→
   128→    const data = db.export()
   129→    writeFileSync(dbPath, Buffer.from(data))
   130→  } finally {
   131→    db.close()
   132→  }
   133→}
   134→

1→/**
     2→ * Transcript discovery — file scanning and project matching.
     3→ * Port of scripts/_transcript_discover.py
     4→ */
     5→
     6→import { existsSync, statSync } from 'node:fs'
     7→import { homedir } from 'node:os'
     8→import { basename, dirname, resolve } from 'node:path'
     9→
    10→import fg from 'fast-glob'
    11→
    12→import type { ParsedTranscript } from '../types/transcript.js'
    13→import { slugify, CLIENTS } from './common.js'
    14→import { toPosixPath } from '../utils/path.js'
    15→
    16→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    17→// Glob patterns per client
    18→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    19→
    20→const CLIENT_PATTERNS: Readonly<Record<string, readonly string[]>> = {
    21→  codex: ['sessions/**/*.jsonl'],
    22→  claude: ['projects/**/*.jsonl'],
    23→  gemini: [
    24→    'tmp/*/checkpoints/**/*.json',
    25→    'tmp/*/checkpoints/**/*.jsonl',
    26→    'history/**/*.json',
    27→    'history/**/*.jsonl',
    28→    'chats/**/*.json',
    29→    'chats/**/*.jsonl',
    30→  ],
    31→}
    32→
    33→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    34→// Defaults
    35→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    36→
    37→export function defaultGlobalTranscriptRoot(): string {
    38→  return toPosixPath(resolve(homedir(), '.memorytree', 'transcripts'))
    39→}
    40→
    41→export function defaultClientRoots(): Record<string, string> {
    42→  const home = homedir()
    43→  return {
    44→    codex: resolve(home, '.codex'),
    45→    claude: resolve(home, '.claude'),
    46→    gemini: resolve(home, '.gemini'),
    47→  }
    48→}
    49→
    50→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    51→// File discovery
    52→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    53→
    54→export function discoverSourceFiles(
    55→  clients?: ReadonlySet<string>,
    56→  roots?: Readonly<Record<string, string>>,
    57→): Array<[string, string]> {
    58→  const requested = clients ?? CLIENTS
    59→  const resolvedRoots = roots ?? defaultClientRoots()
    60→  const matches: Array<[string, string]> = []
    61→  const seen = new Set<string>()
    62→
    63→  for (const client of [...requested].sort()) {
    64→    const root: string | undefined = resolvedRoots[client]
    65→    if (root === undefined || !existsSync(root)) continue
    66→
    67→    const patterns = CLIENT_PATTERNS[client]
    68→    if (patterns === undefined) continue
    69→
    70→    for (const pattern of patterns) {
    71→      const paths = fg.sync(pattern, { cwd: root, absolute: true, onlyFiles: true })
    72→      for (const filePath of paths) {
    73→        const resolved = toPosixPath(resolve(filePath))
    74→        const key = resolved.toLowerCase()
    75→        if (seen.has(key)) continue
    76→        seen.add(key)
    77→        matches.push([client, resolved])
    78→      }
    79→    }
    80→  }
    81→
    82→  matches.sort((a, b) => {
    83→    const mtimeA = safeFileMtime(a[1])
    84→    const mtimeB = safeFileMtime(b[1])
    85→    if (mtimeB !== mtimeA) return mtimeB - mtimeA
    86→    const pathA = a[1].toLowerCase()
    87→    const pathB = b[1].toLowerCase()
    88→    // Reverse sort: higher path string comes first (matching Python's reverse=True)
    89→    if (pathB > pathA) return 1
    90→    if (pathB < pathA) return -1
    91→    return 0
    92→  })
    93→
    94→  return matches
    95→}
    96→
    97→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
    98→// Project slug inference
    99→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   100→
   101→export function inferProjectSlug(parsed: ParsedTranscript): string {
   102→  if (parsed.cwd) {
   103→    try {
   104→      const cwdBase = basename(parsed.cwd)
   105→      return slugify(cwdBase, 'project')
   106→    } catch {
   107→      // fall through
   108→    }
   109→  }
   110→
   111→  if (parsed.client === 'claude') {
   112→    let parentName = basename(dirname(parsed.source_path)).toLowerCase()
   113→    parentName = parentName.replace(/^[a-z]--/, '')
   114→    return slugify(parentName, 'project')
   115→  }
   116→
   117→  if (parsed.client === 'gemini') {
   118→    const parentDir = basename(dirname(parsed.source_path))
   119→    if (parentDir) {
   120→      return slugify(parentDir, 'project')
   121→    }
   122→  }
   123→
   124→  return 'unknown-project'
   125→}
   126→
   127→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   128→// Repo matching
   129→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   130→
   131→export function transcriptMatchesRepo(
   132→  parsed: ParsedTranscript,
   133→  repoRoot: string,
   134→  repoSlug: string,
   135→): boolean {
   136→  const normalizedRepo = toPosixPath(resolve(repoRoot)).toLowerCase()
   137→
   138→  if (parsed.cwd) {
   139→    try {
   140→      const normalizedCwd = toPosixPath(resolve(parsed.cwd)).toLowerCase()
   141→      if (normalizedCwd === normalizedRepo) return true
   142→      // Check if repoRoot is a parent of cwd
   143→      const repoPrefix = normalizedRepo.endsWith('/')
   144→        ? normalizedRepo
   145→        : normalizedRepo + '/'
   146→      if (normalizedCwd.startsWith(repoPrefix)) return true
   147→    } catch {
   148→      // fall through
   149→    }
   150→  }
   151→
   152→  return projectSlugsMatch(inferProjectSlug(parsed), repoSlug)
   153→}
   154→
   155→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   156→// Slug matching
   157→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   158→
   159→export function projectSlugsMatch(left: string, right: string): boolean {
   160→  return Boolean(
   161→    left && right && left === right && left !== 'unknown-project',
   162→  )
   163→}
   164→
   165→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   166→// Safe mtime
   167→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   168→
   169→export function safeFileMtime(filePath: string): number {
   170→  try {
   171→    return statSync(filePath).mtimeMs / 1000
   172→  } catch {
   173→    return 0
   174→  }
   175→}
   176→

    21→export async function importTranscript(

    25→  projectSlug: string,

    47→  const globalManifestPath = join(globalRoot, 'index', 'manifests', parsed.client, projectSlug, yearToken, monthToken, `${artifactStem}.json`)
    48→  const globalEventLogPath = join(globalRoot, 'index', 'sessions.jsonl')

   109→export function transcriptHasContent(parsed: ParsedTranscript): boolean {

   112→
   113→export function writeCleanMarkdown(parsed: ParsedTranscript, manifest: ManifestEntry, filePath: string): void {
   114→  const lines: string[] = [
   115→    '---',
   116→    `client: ${manifest.client}`,
   117→    `project: ${manifest.project}`,
   118→    `session_id: ${manifest.session_id}`,
   119→    `title: ${yamlEscape(manifest.title)}`,
   120→    `started_at: ${manifest.started_at}`,
   121→    `imported_at: ${manifest.imported_at}`,

   125→    `raw_sha256: ${manifest.raw_sha256}`,
   126→    `raw_upload_permission: ${manifest.raw_upload_permission}`,
   127→    `cleaning_mode: ${manifest.cleaning_mode}`,
   128→    '---',
   129→    '',
   130→    `# ${manifest.title || manifest.session_id}`,
   131→    '',
   132→    '## Metadata',
   133→    `- Client: \`${manifest.client}\``,
   134→    `- Project: \`${manifest.project}\``,
   135→    `- Session ID: \`${manifest.session_id}\``,
   136→    `- Started At: \`${manifest.started_at}\``,
   137→    `- Imported At: \`${manifest.imported_at}\``,
   138→    `- Raw SHA256: \`${manifest.raw_sha256}\``,
   139→    `- Raw Source: \`${manifest.raw_source_path}\``,
   140→    `- Repo Raw Path: \`${manifest.repo_raw_path}\``,
   141→    `- Repo Clean Path: \`${manifest.repo_clean_path}\``,
   142→    '',
   143→    '## Messages',
   144→  ]
   145→
   146→  if (parsed.messages.length > 0) {
   147→    for (let i = 0; i < parsed.messages.length; i++) {
   148→      const message = parsed.messages[i]!
   149→      lines.push(
   150→        `### ${i + 1}. ${message.role}`,
   151→        `- Timestamp: \`${message.timestamp ?? manifest.started_at}\``,
   152→        '',
   153→        message.text,
   154→        '',
   155→      )
   156→    }
   157→  } else {
   158→    lines.push(
   159→      'No user or assistant messages were extracted deterministically from the source transcript.',
   160→      '',
   161→    )
   162→  }
   163→
   164→  lines.push('## Tool Events')
   165→  if (parsed.tool_events.length > 0) {
   166→    for (const event of parsed.tool_events) {
   167→      lines.push(`- \`${event.timestamp ?? manifest.started_at}\` ${event.summary}`)
   168→    }
   169→  } else {
   170→    lines.push('- No tool events were extracted.')
   171→  }
   172→  lines.push('')

   174→  writeFileSync(filePath, lines.join('\n'), 'utf-8')

   176→
   177→export function preserveExistingImportTimestamp(
   178→  existing: Record<string, unknown> | null,
   179→  payload: ManifestEntry,
   180→): ManifestEntry {
   181→  if (existing === null) return payload
   182→  if (!deepEqual(manifestSignature(existing), manifestSignature(payload))) return payload
   183→  const importedAt = existing['imported_at']
   184→  if (typeof importedAt === 'string' && importedAt) {
   185→    return { ...payload, imported_at: importedAt }
   186→  }
   187→  return payload
   188→}
   189→
   190→export function manifestSignature(payload: Record<string, unknown>): Record<string, unknown> {
   191→  const result: Record<string, unknown> = {}
   192→  for (const [key, value] of Object.entries(payload)) {
   193→    if (key !== 'imported_at') {
   194→      result[key] = value
   195→    }
   196→  }
   197→  return result
   198→}
   199→
   200→export function manifestChanged(filePath: string, payload: Record<string, unknown>): boolean {
   201→  if (!existsSync(filePath)) return true
   202→  try {
   203→    const current = JSON.parse(readFileSync(filePath, 'utf-8')) as Record<string, unknown>
   204→    return !deepEqual(current, payload)
   205→  } catch {
   206→    return true
   207→  }
   208→}
   209→
   210→export function writeJson(filePath: string, payload: Record<string, unknown>): void {
   211→  writeFileSync(filePath, JSON.stringify(payload, null, 2) + '\n', 'utf-8')
   212→}
   213→
   214→export function appendJsonl(filePath: string, payload: Record<string, unknown>): void {
   215→  appendFileSync(filePath, JSON.stringify(payload) + '\n', 'utf-8')
   216→}
   217→
   218→export function copyFile(source: string, destination: string): void {
   219→  if (source === destination) return
   220→  copyFileSync(source, destination)
   221→}
   222→
   223→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   224→// Internal helpers
   225→// ---------------------------------------------------------------------------
   226→
   227→function stemOf(filePath: string): string {
   228→  const base = filePath.replace(/\\/g, '/').split('/').pop() ?? ''
   229→  const ext = extname(base)
   230→  return ext ? base.slice(0, -ext.length) : base
   231→}
   232→
   233→function deepEqual(a: unknown, b: unknown): boolean {
   234→  if (a === b) return true
   235→  if (a === null || b === null || typeof a !== typeof b) return false
   236→  if (typeof a !== 'object') return false
   237→  if (Array.isArray(a) !== Array.isArray(b)) return false
   238→  const aObj = a as Record<string, unknown>
   239→  const bObj = b as Record<string, unknown>
   240→  const aKeys = Object.keys(aObj)
   241→  const bKeys = Object.keys(bObj)
   242→  if (aKeys.length !== bKeys.length) return false
   243→  return aKeys.every(key => deepEqual(aObj[key], bObj[key]))
   244→}

content.text: Now let me update the project memory:

content.content:
1→# memorytree-workflow — Project Memory
     2→
     3→## Project Overview
     4→
     5→MemoryTree Skill implementation repo (`beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow`). Provides transcript import, dedup, indexing, and session continuity for AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI).
     6→
     7→## Key Architecture
     8→
     9→- **Scripts dir**: `scripts/` — all transcript processing modules
    10→- **Tests**: 7 test files in `tests/`, 231 tests total, 80% coverage
    11→  - `test_transcript_utils.py` — transcript parsing/import (76 tests)
    12→  - `test_heartbeat_modules.py` — P0 config/lock/alert/log (27 tests)
    13→  - `test_integration.py` — end-to-end + heartbeat function-level (27 tests)
    14→  - `test_daemon.py` — memorytree_daemon.py install/uninstall/status/helpers (21 tests)
    15→  - `test_scaffold_utils.py`, `test_locale_utils.py`, `test_upgrade_checks.py`
    16→- **Python**: No local install; use `uv run python` to execute (Windows Store python stub returns exit code 49). Coverage installed via `uv tool install coverage`.
    17→- **Module structure** (P1 split, commit 2aea9da):
    18→  - `_transcript_common.py` — constants, utilities, text extraction, dedup helpers
    19→  - `_transcript_parse.py` — dataclasses + 3 client parsers (Codex/Claude/Gemini)
    20→  - `_transcript_import.py` — import, clean, manifest
    21→  - `_transcript_index.py` — SQLite index
    22→  - `_transcript_discover.py` — file discovery, project matching
    23→  - `_transcript_utils.py` — backward-compatible re-export hub
    24→- **P0 heartbeat modules** (code complete, tests done):
    25→  - `_config_utils.py` — TOML config load/save, interval parsing, project registration
    26→  - `_lock_utils.py` — PID-based lock file with stale detection
    27→  - `_alert_utils.py` — alerts.json management, dedup, threshold-based writes
    28→  - `_log_utils.py` — logging setup with file rotation + stderr
    29→  - `heartbeat.py` — main heartbeat logic, sensitive pattern scanning
    30→  - `memorytree_daemon.py` — CLI entry point (install/uninstall/run-once/watch)
    31→
    32→## Completed Work
    33→
    34→### P1: Transcript dedup (commit 9091b33)
    35→- `deduplicate_messages()` / `deduplicate_tool_events()` in `_transcript_common.py`
    36→- Signature: `(timestamp, role, sha256_prefix)` for messages, `(timestamp, sha256_prefix)` for tool events
    37→- sha256 prefix = 16-char hex (`hashlib.sha256(text.encode()).hexdigest()[:16]`)
    38→- All 3 parsers call dedup before building `ParsedTranscript`
    39→- Gemini's inline `message_signatures` set removed in favor of shared dedup
    40→- 17 new tests (7 message unit, 4 tool event unit, 6 parser integration)
    41→
    42→### P1: Module split (commit 2aea9da)
    43→- Split monolithic `_transcript_utils.py` into 5 focused modules (all ≤400 lines)
    44→
    45→### P1: CI pipeline (commit 410da8b)
    46→- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — triggered on push/PR to master
    47→- Matrix: Python 3.11/3.12/3.13 × ubuntu/macos/windows (9 combos)
    48→- `coverage run -m unittest discover -s tests` + `--fail-under=80`
    49→- ubuntu+3.13 uploads `coverage.xml` artifact
    50→
    51→### P1: Integration tests (commit bebbc0f, expanded feb18cb)
    52→- `tests/test_integration.py` — 7 test classes, 27 tests
    53→- Scenario 1: init → parse → import e2e

# memorytree-workflow — Project Memory

## Project Overview

MemoryTree Skill implementation repo (`beyondchenlin/memorytree-workflow`). Provides transcript import, dedup, indexing, and session continuity for AI coding assistants (Claude Code, Codex, Gemini CLI).

## Key Architecture

- **Scripts dir**: `scripts/` — all transcript processing modules
- **Tests**: 7 test files in `tests/`, 231 tests total, 80% coverage
  - `test_transcript_utils.py` — transcript parsing/import (76 tests)
  - `test_heartbeat_modules.py` — P0 config/lock/alert/log (27 tests)
  - `test_integration.py` — end-to-end + heartbeat function-level (27 tests)
  - `test_daemon.py` — memorytree_daemon.py install/uninstall/status/helpers (21 tests)
  - `test_scaffold_utils.py`, `test_locale_utils.py`, `test_upgrade_checks.py`
- **Python**: No local install; use `uv run python` to execute (Windows Store python stub returns exit code 49). Coverage installed via `uv tool install coverage`.
- **Module structure** (P1 split, commit 2aea9da):
  - `_transcript_common.py` — constants, utilities, text extraction, dedup helpers
  - `_transcript_parse.py` — dataclasses + 3 client parsers (Codex/Claude/Gemini)
  - `_transcript_import.py` — import, clean, manifest
  - `_transcript_index.py` — SQLite index
  - `_transcript_discover.py` — file discovery, project matching
  - `_transcript_utils.py` — backward-compatible re-export hub
- **P0 heartbeat modules** (code complete, tests done):
  - `_config_utils.py` — TOML config load/save, interval parsing, project registration
  - `_lock_utils.py` — PID-based lock file with stale detection
  - `_alert_utils.py` — alerts.json management, dedup, threshold-based writes
  - `_log_utils.py` — logging setup with file rotation + stderr
  - `heartbeat.py` — main heartbeat logic, sensitive pattern scanning
  - `memorytree_daemon.py` — CLI entry point (install/uninstall/run-once/watch)

## Completed Work

### P1: Transcript dedup (commit 9091b33)
- `deduplicate_messages()` / `deduplicate_tool_events()` in `_transcript_common.py`
- Signature: `(timestamp, role, sha256_prefix)` for messages, `(timestamp, sha256_prefix)` for tool events
- sha256 prefix = 16-char hex (`hashlib.sha256(text.encode()).hexdigest()[:16]`)
- All 3 parsers call dedup before building `ParsedTranscript`
- Gemini's inline `message_signatures` set removed in favor of shared dedup
- 17 new tests (7 message unit, 4 tool event unit, 6 parser integration)

### P1: Module split (commit 2aea9da)
- Split monolithic `_transcript_utils.py` into 5 focused modules (all ≤400 lines)

### P1: CI pipeline (commit 410da8b)
- `.github/workflows/ci.yml` — triggered on push/PR to master
- Matrix: Python 3.11/3.12/3.13 × ubuntu/macos/windows (9 combos)
- `coverage run -m unittest discover -s tests` + `--fail-under=80`
- ubuntu+3.13 uploads `coverage.xml` artifact

### P1: Integration tests (commit bebbc0f, expanded feb18cb)
- `tests/test_integration.py` — 7 test classes, 27 tests
- Scenario 1: init → parse → import e2e
- Scenario 2: discover batch scan (3 client layouts)
- Scenario 3: heartbeat sensitive pattern detection
- Scenario 4: heartbeat function-level tests (main, _run_heartbeat, _process_project, _git_commit_and_push, _try_push, _git)
- `heartbeat.py` coverage: 93% (up from 25%)

### P0: Daemon unit tests (commit 2a31d80)
- `tests/test_daemon.py` — 21 tests
- cmd_install: Linux/macOS/Windows dispatch, --interval/--auto-push overrides, duplicate rejection
- cmd_uninstall: 3 platforms (cron filter, launchctl unload, schtasks /delete)
- cmd_run_once, cmd_status (registered+locked / unregistered+unlocked)
- Helpers: _heartbeat_script_path, _python_path (with/without uv)
- Platform detection: _is_cron/launchd/schtasks_registered (positive + negative)
- `memorytree_daemon.py` coverage: 72%

### P0: Heartbeat module unit tests (commit c8c5d28)
- `tests/test_heartbeat_modules.py` — 27 tests
- Covers _config_utils, _lock_utils, _alert_utils, _log_utils
- Windows caveat: logging FileHandler holds file locks; must close handlers before TemporaryDirectory cleanup

### P0.5: Session continuity (commit a47dea9)
- Cross-client context restore

## TODO Status

See `TODO.md` for full details. Summary:
- P0 (heartbeat daemon): code complete, all tests done (heartbeat modules + daemon + integration); only "first install trigger" (pure AI instruction) remains unchecked
- P0.5 (session continuity): done
- P1 (code quality): all done (dedup, module split, CI pipeline, integration tests)
- P2/P3: blocked on P0

<result>Both files are complete and all tests pass. Here is a summary:

## Implementation

**`D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\src\transcript\db.ts`** -- Port of `_transcript_index.py`:
- Exports `upsertSearchIndex(dbPath, manifest)` returning `Promise<void>`
- Uses sql.js with a lazily cached WASM init promise (`getSqlJs()`)
- Opens existing db file or creates new one
- Runs `PRAGMA journal_mode=WAL`, creates the `transcripts` table (18 columns, composite PK of 4), then performs `INSERT ... ON CONFLICT DO UPDATE SET` on the 14 non-PK data columns
- Exports the in-memory db to disk via `writeFileSync(dbPath, Buffer.from(db.export()))` and closes in a `try/finally`
- Parameter type is `ManifestEntry` (from `src/types/transcript.js`) rather than `Record<string, unknown>` to avoid strict-mode index-signature incompatibility
- Column lists (`PK_COLUMNS`, `DATA_COLUMNS`, `ALL_COLUMNS`) are defined as `as const` arrays and used to build SQL strings, avoiding duplication

**`D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\tests\transcript\db.test.ts`** -- 6 tests, all passing:
1. Creates a new db file and inserts one record, verified by reading back with sql.js
2. Upsert -- inserts then updates same PK, verifies updated fields and single row
3. Table schema has exactly 18 columns with correct names
4. Inserts 3 different records, verifies all present
5. Opens existing db and preserves previous records across calls
6. Composite primary key enforcement (same session_id, different raw_sha256 = 2 rows)

**Additional change**: Installed `@types/sql.js` as a dev dependency to satisfy strict type checking.</result>

Here is a summary of what was implemented:

**Implementation file: `D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\src\transcript\parse.ts`**

Ported the full Python `_transcript_parse.py` module to TypeScript, exporting 5 functions:

1. **`inferClient(client, sourcePath)`** - Auto-detects client from path patterns using `toPosixPath` for normalization. Matches `/.codex/`, `rollout-`, `/.claude/`, `/projects/`, `/.gemini/`, `checkpoint`. Throws `Error` (not `SystemExit`) for unrecognized paths.

2. **`parseTranscript(client, sourcePath)`** - Dispatches to the appropriate parser after calling `inferClient`.

3. **`parseCodexTranscript(filePath)`** - JSONL parser handling `session_meta`, `response_item`/`event_msg` records with all payload types (`message`, `user_message`/`agent_message`, `function_call`/`custom_tool_call` and their output variants).

4. **`parseClaudeTranscript(filePath)`** - JSONL parser extracting `sessionId`, `cwd`, `gitBranch` from each record, processing content blocks (text, tool_use, tool_result, skipping thinking/reasoning).

5. **`parseGeminiTranscript(filePath)`** - JSON/JSONL parser with recursive `visit()` traversal, metadata extraction via `findFirstMappingWithKeys`, role normalization (`model` -> `assistant`), and tool detection from `toolUse.name`/`toolName`/`functionName`/`tool`.

All parsers apply `deduplicateMessages` and `deduplicateToolEvents` before returning.

**Test file: `D:\demo1\memorytree-workflow\tests\transcript\parse.test.ts`**

35 tests across 5 describe blocks:
- `inferClient` (9 tests): explicit client, all 3 auto-detect patterns, case insensitivity, error on unknown
- `parseCodexTranscript` (7 tests): session_meta + messages, user_message/agent_message, function_call/output, custom_tool_call/output, dedup, fallback IDs, unknown record skipping
- `parseClaudeTranscript` (7 tests): text blocks, string content, tool_use/tool_result, thinking skip, non-user/assistant skip, dedup, string blocks in arrays
- `parseGeminiTranscript` (8 tests): JSON file, JSONL file, toolUse.name, toolName, metadata extraction, dedup, fallback session_id, author field
- `parseTranscript` (4 tests): codex/claude/gemini dispatch, auto-infer from path</result>

content.content:
1→---
     2→name: memorytree-workflow
     3→description: Detect, initialize, and maintain a reusable MemoryTree project-memory workspace for any code repository. On activation, immediately detect and report the repo's MemoryTree state.
     4→---
     5→
     6→# MemoryTree
     7→
     8→Use this skill for repo-level project memory. Do not treat it as an application runtime feature unless the user explicitly asks to productize it.
     9→
    10→## On Activation
    11→
    12→When this skill is invoked, immediately perform these steps without waiting for further user input:
    13→
    14→1. Detect the repo state (`not-installed`, `partial`, or `installed`) using `references/project-detection.md`.
    15→2. Check `~/.memorytree/alerts.json`. If pending alerts exist, display them to the user before proceeding.
    16→3. If `not-installed`: run `scripts/init-memorytree.py` to scaffold the workspace, then report what was created.
    17→4. If `partial`: run `scripts/upgrade-memorytree.py` to add missing pieces, then report what was added.
    18→5. If `installed`: read the active goal, bound todo, and latest chat log. Report the current state (goal summary, todo progress, pending tasks) and ask the user what they want to work on next. If `memorytree-daemon` is available but not registered, ask the user for their preferred heartbeat interval, auto_push preference, and scope, then offer to run `memorytree-daemon install`. Use plain-text questions compatible with all AI assistants, not client-specific UI components.
    19→
    20→Do not stop after detection to ask whether you should proceed. Act on the result immediately.
    21→
    22→## Core Behavior
    23→
    24→1. Detect the repo state before changing files. Use `references/project-detection.md`.
    25→2. If MemoryTree is missing, initialize it with `scripts/init-memorytree.py`. If the script cannot run, create the files from `assets/templates/`.
    26→3. If MemoryTree is partial, upgrade it with `scripts/upgrade-memorytree.py` before manual maintenance.
    27→4. If MemoryTree is already installed, maintain only the active goal, bound todo, and current chat log. Use `references/update-rules.md`.
    28→5. Keep the installation compatible with the host repo's branch, PR, CI, and review rules. Use `references/git-policy.md`.
    29→6. Prefer safe defaults: do not add reply suffix requirements unless the host repository already requires them, do not mix code-and-memory commits by default, and do not override branch policy. MemoryTree-only git automation is allowed only inside the isolated flow in `references/git-policy.md`.
    30→7. When locale is not explicit, choose it from repo language first, then system language. Use `references/locale-selection.md`.
    31→8. Keep reply language aligned with user language first, then repo locale. Use `references/response-language.md`.
    32→9. For partial repos, use the non-destructive upgrade path before manual maintenance. Use `references/upgrade-path.md`.
    33→10. When transcript archival is enabled, import transcripts with deterministic code, preserve raw transcripts as evidence in both the current repo mirror and the global archive, generate cleaned Markdown for indexing, keep cross-project backfills and ambiguous ownership cases in the global archive only, and ask once per repository whether raw transcript files may be committed or pushed. Use `references/transcript-archive.md`.
    34→11. When scripts are needed, prefer an existing `uv run python` environment first, then a system `python`, and only fall back to manual scaffolding or conservative manual review if no runner is available. Use `references/execution-environment.md`.
    35→12. Transcript discovery, import, cleaning, and push are handled by the background heartbeat process. The model only writes chat log summaries and updates goals and todos. See `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.
    36→13. Global configuration is stored in `~/.memorytree/config.toml`. See `references/global-configuration.md`.
    37→14. When the user asks to see their most recent conversation, trigger an on-demand transcript sync for the current project, locate the latest session across all three clients, and generate a continuation summary. See `references/transcript-archive.md` and `references/heartbeat-scheduling.md`.
